expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: search box selector dropdown planning covers OK or Cancel confirmation, pending selection, and dismissal outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Explicit coverage of benchmark focus…”, the plan includes scenarios: “Confirm selection with OK button”, “Cancel does not commit changes”, “Dismissal via outside click matches Cancel semantics”, and “Pending selection (debounce/loading) does not cause unexpected popover dismissal” (plus “OK is gated correctly when pending selection is not yet stable”). These explicitly cover OK/Cancel confirmation, pending selection, and dismissal outcomes.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states “Phase alignment: Phase 4a (subcategory-only draft)” and structures content as “subcategory → scenario → atomic steps → observable verifications,” noting Phase 4a constraints (e.g., “forbids canonical top-layer grouping”). The listed items are written as subcategory scenarios with nested steps and verification leaves, consistent with phase4a.
